log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable with tags indicating persistence, overlay, spreader, and runtime modules. The network activity shows C2 communication to carder.bit and ransomware.bit, with POST reques

The IOC summary confirms 2 unique IPs, 5 domains, 3 dropped files, and 3 registry persistence keys. The mutexes were not counted in this summary but were visible in the initial report with 'ransom_id'

The network activity confirms C2 communication with 110.110.110.3 via POST requests with obfuscated parameters, and DNS lookups for carder.bit and ransomware.bit. The sample also queries ipv4bot.whati

The dropped files are placed in AppData\Roaming\Microsoft and the current directory, mimicking legitimate Microsoft executables. This is a common technique for ransomware to hide in plain sight. I nee

The registry persistence keys confirm the ransomware behavior, with RunOnce entries pointing to the dropped executables (ofzgta.exe, iesptp.exe). The sample also modifies Internet Settings registry ke
